mnemonics: PRIORITY QUEUES: PQ = Prioritize Quickly. Think of a hospital ER (triage) or a to-do list where the most urgent task is always next.
keyPoints: A Priority Queue is an abstract data type where each element has a 'priority'. The element with the highest (or lowest) priority is served first, regardless of the order in which it was added.
Heaps (specifically Binary Heaps) are the most common and efficient data structure used to implement a Priority Queue, allowing for O(log n) insertion and extraction of the max/min element.

Unlike a standard FIFO queue, the dequeue operation removes the element based on priority, not the order of arrival. This is crucial for applications like task scheduling, Dijkstra's algorithm, and Huffman coding.
commonMistakes: Assuming it is implemented as a simple sorted or unsorted array, which leads to inefficient O(n) operations for insertion or extraction.
Confusing a Heap (the implementation) with the Priority Queue (the abstract interface). A Priority Queue defines the behavior; a Heap is one way to achieve it.
Forgetting to specify whether it is a Max-Heap (for highest priority first) or a Min-Heap (for lowest priority first), which leads to incorrect results.
